batch_summary: Deed [305] PDF pulled (ISSUE-43 item 23). Instrument is a RELEASE OF DAMAGE CLAIMS by J. P. Lindly against the Houston & Texas Central Railroad Company — NOT a reconveyance of land as the LC index direction ('H&TC RAILROAD CO → LINDLY J P') implied. Lindly released the railroad from all past and future overflow/damage claims on his 103-ac McAnulty League tract for $100. Key finding: upstream chain confirms H. Clay Dunbar (deed [123] grantee, 200 ac from JH Bounds, 1869) conveyed 103 ac of his McAnulty purchase to J. M. Lindly in 1870 (Book H pp. 338-339 LC), who conveyed to J. P. Lindly in 1884 (Vol. W pp. 498-499 LC). J. P. Lindly = J. P. Lindley of deeds [101]/[105] (name variant confirmed). J. M. Lindly and wife A. Lindly are newly identified persons. No land changed hands in this instrument; no mineral impact; no conveyance. ISSUE-43 item 23 satisfied. ISSUE-34 advanced (Dunbar downstream chain partially documented). File naming discrepancy noted: filename uses '_f_' (Freestone) but LC index identifies instrument at LC Vol. 34 Pg. 256 — recording county needs human verification.
